---
title: "testing.Rmd"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-29"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
pkgload::load_all()

# Identify yourself to NCBI and keep manual-test downloads separate from
# your normal package cache.
Sys.setenv(
  ENTREZ_EMAIL = "ryanreis333@gmail.com",
  SCGEO_CACHE = file.path(tempdir(), "R2scGEO-test-cache")
)
```

```{r smoke-test-search}
hits <- scgeo_search("glioblastoma", organism = "Homo sapiens", max_results = 5)
hits
```

```{r smoke-test-metadata}
meta <- scgeo_metadata("GSE164897")
meta
```

```{r smoke-test-supplementary-files}
files <- scgeo_suppl_files("GSE164897")
files
```

```{r smoke-test-small-download}
small <- scgeo_download("GSE164897", pattern = "filelist", overwrite = TRUE)
small
```

```{r inspect-cache}
scgeo_cache_dir()
list.files(scgeo_cache_dir(), recursive = TRUE)
```

```{r raw-download-disabled, eval=FALSE}
# Only run this after inspecting `files`; GEO RAW archives can be large.
raw <- scgeo_download("GSE164897", pattern = "RAW", overwrite = FALSE)
raw

# Start with one sample so you are not parsing every dense count file at once.
probe3 <- scgeo_load(dirname(raw[1]), as = "matrix", sample = "GSM5022595")
probe3
dim(probe3)
probe3[1:5, 1:5]

# When the one-sample path looks good, this loads all four GSM matrices.
all_samples <- scgeo_load(dirname(raw[1]), as = "matrix")
names(all_samples)
vapply(all_samples, dim, integer(2))
```
